short_summary: Iran deal may ease energy costs
rationale: The Iran-US deal inching closer is the most relevant development for Portugal, as lower global energy prices would ease cost-of-living pressures; no other headline directly affects Portuguese daily life.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to peace deal
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal could reduce global oil and gas prices, offering indirect relief to Portuguese consumers and businesses still dealing with elevated energy costs.

top_events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from climate-hit countries
top_events.summary: US immigration crackdowns on nationals from climate-vulnerable countries could affect some Portuguese-speaking communities (e.g. Cape Verde, Brazil diaspora links), but direct impact on Portugal is minimal.

top_events.title: Global brands linked to DRC conflict minerals
top_events.summary: Investigation into conflict minerals in global supply chains has marginal relevance to Portugal's consumer economy — low direct impact.
